content_markdown: Two waves meet at a point. The waves have a path difference of $\frac{\lambda}{4}$. The phase difference between the waves is

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: The relationship between phase difference ($\phi$) and path difference ($\Delta x$) is given by the formula $\phi = \frac{2\pi}{\lambda} \Delta x$. Substituting the given path difference $\Delta x = \frac{\lambda}{4}$ into the formula gives $\phi = \frac{2\pi}{\lambda} \cdot \frac{\lambda}{4} = \frac{2\pi}{4} = \frac{\pi}{2}$ radians.
